en: Technology, the thing that will eventually make television so high-def I will no longer be allowed to appear on it.
ko: 기술. 결국 TV가 너무 고화질이 되면 제가 출연 못 하게 될 거예요.

en: And I want to approach this subject carefully because discussions of any new technology tend to age very badly, as this news report from less than five years ago shows.
ko: 이 주제를 조심스럽게 접근하려 해요. 신기술 논의는 금방 낡아버리거든요. 5년도 안 된 뉴스가 이걸 보여줘요.

en: The first users of Google glasses surely will not be the last.
ko: 구글 글래스의 첫 사용자가 마지막은 아닐 거예요.

en: I think it will end up changing our lifestyles, you know, changing the way we interact with each other and just changing the gesture.
ko: 우리 생활방식을 바꾸고 상호작용 방식을 바꾸고 제스처를 바꿀 거라 생각해요.

en: Instead of this, it will be that. Yeah, it wasn't though, it was not that, because for some reason people didn't mind doing this with their hands if the alternative was wearing a stupid robot on their face all day.
ko: 이게 아니라 저게 될 거라고요. 하지만 아니었어요. 얼굴에 바보 로봇 달고 다니느니 손으로 이렇게 하는 게 나으니까요.

en: The point is, it is dangerous to make predictions about where tech is going.
ko: 요점은 기술의 미래를 예측하는 건 위험하다는 거예요.

en: So that said, tonight we're going to talk about cryptocurrencies, everything you don't understand about money combined with everything you don't understand about computers.
ko: 그래서 오늘은 암호화폐 얘기예요. 돈에 대해 모르는 것과 컴퓨터에 대해 모르는 것의 조합이죠.
